title: Acting can be developed at any age: Delhi High Court stays 30-year age cap for NSD admissions
description: The Delhi High Court recently stayed the admission criteria for the National School of Drama (NSD) that barred applicants above the age of 30 from applying for its flagship Dramatic Arts course. Justice Jasmeet Singh reasoned that acting is an art that can be developed and refined at any stage of life, and fixing the upper age limit is arbitrary, unreasonable and against Articles 14, 19(1)(g), and 21 of the Constitution of India.
